words.definitionEn: a person whose job involves examining facts or materials in order to give an opinion on them
words.exampleEn: a political/financial analyst

words.definitionEn: a person in your family who lived a long time ago
words.exampleEn: His ancestors had come to America from Ireland.

words.definitionEn: to supply something with material that can be burnt to produce heat or power
words.exampleEn: Uranium is used to fuel nuclear plants.

words.definitionEn: to do or achieve what was hoped for or expected
words.exampleEn: to fulfil your dream/ambition/potential

words.definitionEn: for all the hours of a week during which people normally work or study, rather than just for a part of it
words.exampleEn: students in full-time education

words.definitionEn: for all the hours of a week during which people normally work or study, rather than just for a part of it
words.exampleEn: students in full-time education

words.definitionEn: completely
words.exampleEn: I fully understand your motives.

words.definitionEn: to work in the correct way
words.exampleEn: We now have a functioning shower.

words.definitionEn: an amount of money that has been saved or has been made available for a particular purpose
words.exampleEn: a disaster relief fund

words.definitionEn: to provide money for something, usually something official
words.exampleEn: Who is funding this research?

words.definitionEn: serious and very important; affecting the most central and important parts of something
words.exampleEn: the fundamental principles of scientific method

words.definitionEn: in every way that is important; completely
words.exampleEn: The two approaches are fundamentally different.

words.definitionEn: money for a particular purpose; the act of providing money for such a purpose
words.exampleEn: federal/state funding

words.definitionEn: very angry
words.exampleEn: Their incompetence made me furious.

words.definitionEn: in addition to what has just been stated. Furthermore is used especially to add a point to an argument.
words.exampleEn: He said he had not discussed the matter with her. Furthermore, he had not even contacted her.

words.definitionEn: an increase in the amount of something, especially in wealth or weight
words.exampleEn: The opposition made unexpected gains in the last election.

words.definitionEn: to obtain or win something, especially something that you need or want
words.exampleEn: gain something, They managed to gain access to secret files.

words.definitionEn: playing computer games
words.exampleEn: online/mobile/console gaming

words.definitionEn: an organized group of criminals
words.exampleEn: criminal gangs

words.definitionEn: sexually attracted to people of the same sex
words.exampleEn: gay men

words.definitionEn: the fact of being male or female, especially when considered with reference to social and cultural differences, rather than differences in biology; members of a particular gender as a group
words.exampleEn: issues of class, race and gender

words.definitionEn: the strong feeling that you have when something has happened that you think is bad and unfair
words.exampleEn: She had to find a way to express her pent-up anger.

words.definitionEn: a unit inside a cell that controls a particular quality in a living thing that has been passed on from its parents
words.exampleEn: a dominant/recessive gene

words.definitionEn: to produce energy, especially electricity
words.exampleEn: The wind turbines are used to generate electricity.

words.definitionEn: connected with genes (= the units in the cells of a living thing that control its physical characteristics) or genetics (= the study of genes)
words.exampleEn: genetic and environmental factors

words.definitionEn: unusually great intelligence, skill or artistic ability
words.exampleEn: the genius of Shakespeare

words.definitionEn: the space between two lines or surfaces that join, measured in degrees
words.exampleEn: a 45° angle

words.definitionEn: a particular type or style of literature, art, film or music that you can recognize because of its special features
words.exampleEn: literary/musical genres

words.definitionEn: real; exactly what it appears to be; not artificial
words.exampleEn: Is the painting a genuine Picasso?

words.definitionEn: truly; in a way that is exactly what it appears to be and is not artificial
words.exampleEn: There are some genuinely funny moments in the film.

words.definitionEn: a movement that you make with your hands, your head or your face to show a particular meaning
words.exampleEn: He made a rude gesture at the driver of the other car.

words.definitionEn: a performance by musicians playing popular music or jazz in front of an audience; a similar performance by a comedian
words.exampleEn: They're doing a gig in Boston tonight.

words.definitionEn: the world (used especially to emphasize its size)
words.exampleEn: tourists from every corner of the globe

words.definitionEn: the process of making films, videos and computer games in which drawings, models or images of people and animals seem to move
words.exampleEn: computer/cartoon animation

words.definitionEn: made of gold
words.exampleEn: a golden crown

words.definitionEn: the quality of being good
words.exampleEn: the essential goodness of human nature

words.definitionEn: very beautiful and attractive; giving great pleasure
words.exampleEn: a gorgeous girl/man

words.definitionEn: to legally control a country or its people and be responsible for introducing new laws, organizing public services, etc.
words.exampleEn: The country is governed by elected representatives of the people.

words.definitionEn: a person who is the official head of a country or region that is governed by another country
words.exampleEn: the former governor of the colony

words.definitionEn: to take or hold somebody/something with your hand suddenly or roughly
words.exampleEn: grab somebody/something, She grabbed his arm as he got up to leave.

words.definitionEn: to give a grade to a student or to a piece of their written work
words.exampleEn: grade somebody/something, I spent all weekend grading papers.

words.definitionEn: a date that is an exact number of years after the date of an important or special event
words.exampleEn: on the anniversary of his wife’s death

words.definitionEn: slowly, over a long period of time
words.exampleEn: to gradually increase/decrease

words.definitionEn: impressive and large or important
words.exampleEn: It's not a very grand house.

words.definitionEn: a sum of money that is given by the government or by another organization to be used for a particular purpose
words.exampleEn: government/federal grants

words.definitionEn: to agree to give somebody what they ask for, especially formal or legal permission to do something
words.exampleEn: grant something, My request was granted.

words.definitionEn: connected with drawings and design, especially in the production of books, magazines, websites, etc.
words.exampleEn: graphic design

words.definitionEn: designs, drawings or pictures that are used especially in the production of books, magazines, websites, etc.
words.exampleEn: Text and graphics are prepared separately and then combined.

words.definitionEn: very much
words.exampleEn: People's reaction to the film has varied greatly.

words.definitionEn: a shop that sells food and other things used in the home. In American English ‘grocery store’ is often used to mean ‘supermarket’.
words.exampleEn: the corner grocery store

words.definitionEn: a formal promise that you will do something or that something will happen
words.exampleEn: They are demanding certain guarantees before they sign the treaty.

words.definitionEn: to promise to do something; to promise something will happen
words.exampleEn: guarantee something, Basic human rights, including freedom of speech, are now guaranteed.

words.definitionEn: a set of rules or instructions that are given by an official organization telling you how to do something, especially something difficult
words.exampleEn: The government has drawn up guidelines for schools during the pandemic.

words.definitionEn: happening or done once every year
words.exampleEn: an annual meeting/event/report/conference

words.definitionEn: the place where a particular type of animal or plant is normally found
words.exampleEn: The panda's natural habitat is the bamboo forest.

words.definitionEn: once a year
words.exampleEn: The exhibition is held annually.

words.definitionEn: the part of a door, window, etc. that you use to open it
words.exampleEn: She turned the handle and opened the door.

words.definitionEn: to deal with a situation, a person, an area of work or a strong emotion
words.exampleEn: handle something/somebody, A new man was appointed to handle the crisis.

words.definitionEn: an area of water on the coast, protected from the open sea by strong walls, where ships can shelter
words.exampleEn: Several boats lay at anchor in the harbour.

words.definitionEn: damage or injury that is caused by a person or an event
words.exampleEn: He would never frighten anyone or cause them any harm.

words.definitionEn: to hurt or injure somebody or to damage something
words.exampleEn: He would never harm anyone.

words.definitionEn: causing damage or injury to somebody/something, especially to a person’s health or to the environment
words.exampleEn: the harmful effects of alcohol

words.definitionEn: a place from which an organization or a military operation is controlled; the people who work there
words.exampleEn: The firm’s headquarters is/are in London.

words.definitionEn: to become healthy again; to make something healthy again
words.exampleEn: It took a long time for the wounds to heal.

words.definitionEn: the service of providing medical care
words.exampleEn: the costs of healthcare for the elderly

words.definitionEn: the ability to hear
words.exampleEn: Her hearing is poor.

words.definitionEn: the place believed to be the home of God where good people go when they die, sometimes imagined to be in the sky
words.exampleEn: the kingdom of heaven

words.definitionEn: the back part of the foot below the ankle
words.exampleEn: Apply the cream to dry, cracked heels before bed.

words.definitionEn: the place believed to be the home of devils and where bad people go after death
words.exampleEn: He was terrified of going to hell when he died.

words.definitionEn: to expect something
words.exampleEn: anticipate something, We don't anticipate any major problems.

words.definitionEn: a type of hard hat that protects the head, worn, for example, by a police officer, a soldier or a person riding a bike or motorbike or playing some sports
words.exampleEn: Police in riot helmets lined the streets.

words.definitionEn: for this reason
words.exampleEn: We suspect they are trying to hide something, hence the need for an independent inquiry.

words.definitionEn: a plant whose leaves, flowers or seeds are used to add taste to food, in medicines or for their pleasant smell. parsley, mint and oregano are all herbs.
words.exampleEn: a herb garden

words.definitionEn: a way of pronouncing the words of a language that shows which country, area or social class a person comes from; how well somebody pronounces a particular language
words.exampleEn: a northern/Dublin/Scottish accent

words.definitionEn: the state of feeling nervous or worried that something bad is going to happen
words.exampleEn: acute/intense/deep anxiety

words.definitionEn: to be slow to speak or act because you feel uncertain or nervous
words.exampleEn: She hesitated before replying.

words.definitionEn: something that is hidden is kept or located in a place where it cannot be seen
words.exampleEn: Hidden dangers lurk in the ocean depths.

words.definitionEn: feeling worried or nervous
words.exampleEn: The bus was late and Sue began to get anxious.

words.definitionEn: the highest level or number
words.exampleEn: to hit/reach a high

words.definitionEn: a main road for travelling long distances, especially one connecting and going through cities and towns
words.exampleEn: Plans were being made for the construction of a new interstate highway system.

words.definitionEn: extremely funny
words.exampleEn: a hilarious joke/story

words.definitionEn: the area at either side of the body between the top of the leg and the middle part of the body; the joint at the top of the leg that connects it with the top part of the body
words.exampleEn: She stood with her hands on her hips.

words.definitionEn: the act of paying to use something for a short time
words.exampleEn: a hire car

words.definitionEn: a person who studies or writes about history; an expert in history
words.exampleEn: No serious historian today accepts this theory.

words.definitionEn: the action of holding somebody/something; the way you are holding somebody/something
words.exampleEn: hold on somebody/something, His hold on her arm tightened.

words.definitionEn: having a hole or empty space inside
words.exampleEn: a hollow ball/centre/tube/tree

words.definitionEn: connected with God or a particular religion
words.exampleEn: the Holy Bible

words.definitionEn: having no home, and therefore typically living on the streets
words.exampleEn: The scheme has been set up to help homeless people.

words.definitionEn: the quality of being honest
words.exampleEn: She answered all my questions with her usual honesty.

words.definitionEn: great respect for somebody
words.exampleEn: the guest of honour (= the most important one)

words.definitionEn: to do something that shows great respect for somebody/something
words.exampleEn: honour somebody/something, our honoured guests

words.definitionEn: a curved piece of metal, plastic or wire for hanging things on, catching fish with, etc.
words.exampleEn: a picture/curtain/coat hook

words.definitionEn: used to express what you hope will happen
words.exampleEn: Hopefully, we'll arrive before dark.

words.definitionEn: to organize an event to which others are invited and make all the arrangements for them
words.exampleEn: to host an event/a conference/a meeting

words.definitionEn: to provide a place for a person or an animal to live
words.exampleEn: The government is committed to housing the refugees.

words.definitionEn: all the people living together in a house or flat
words.exampleEn: Most households now own at least one car.

words.definitionEn: houses, flats, etc. that people live in, especially when referring to their type, price or condition
words.exampleEn: rental/student housing

words.definitionEn: funny; showing a sense of humour
words.exampleEn: He gave a humorous account of their trip to Spain.

words.definitionEn: the quality in something that makes it funny; the ability to laugh at things that are funny
words.exampleEn: It was a story full of gentle humour.

words.definitionEn: the state of not having enough food to eat, especially when this causes illness or death
words.exampleEn: Around fifty people die of hunger every day in the camp.

words.definitionEn: an act of looking for somebody/something that is difficult to find
words.exampleEn: The hunt is on for a suitable candidate.

words.definitionEn: going after and killing wild animals as a sport or for food
words.exampleEn: He goes hunting every weekend.

words.definitionEn: a feeling of unhappiness because somebody has been unkind or unfair to you
words.exampleEn: There was hurt and real anger in her voice.

words.definitionEn: an idea or explanation of something that is based on a few known facts but that has not yet been proved to be true or correct
words.exampleEn: to formulate/confirm a hypothesis

words.definitionEn: a small symbol on a computer or smartphone screen that represents a program or a file
words.exampleEn: Click on the printer icon with the mouse.

words.definitionEn: an official way of showing who you are, for example a document with your name, date of birth and often a photograph on it (the abbreviation for ‘identity’ or ‘identification’)
words.exampleEn: You must carry ID at all times.

words.definitionEn: an idea or standard that seems perfect, and worth trying to achieve or obtain
words.exampleEn: He was accused of betraying his political ideals.

words.definitionEn: similar in every detail
words.exampleEn: a row of identical houses

words.definitionEn: a false idea or belief, especially about somebody or about a situation
words.exampleEn: under the illusion that…, She's under the illusion that (= believes wrongly that) she'll get the job.

words.definitionEn: to use pictures, photographs, diagrams, etc. in a book, etc.
words.exampleEn: illustrate something, She illustrated her own books.

words.definitionEn: a drawing or picture in a book, magazine, for decoration or to explain something
words.exampleEn: 50 full-colour illustrations

words.definitionEn: the ability to create pictures in your mind; the part of your mind that does this
words.exampleEn: a vivid/fertile imagination

words.definitionEn: the process of coming to live permanently in a different country from the one you were born in; the number of people who do this
words.exampleEn: legal/illegal immigration

words.definitionEn: that cannot catch or be affected by a particular disease or illness
words.exampleEn: Adults are often immune to German measles.

words.definitionEn: annoyed by somebody/something, especially because you have to wait for a long time
words.exampleEn: I'd been waiting for twenty minutes and I was getting impatient.

words.definitionEn: to make something that has been officially decided start to happen or be used
words.exampleEn: to implement changes/decisions/policies/reforms

words.definitionEn: a possible effect or result of an action or a decision
words.exampleEn: They failed to consider the wider implications of their actions.

words.definitionEn: to suggest that something is true or that you feel or think something, without saying so directly
words.exampleEn: imply (that)…, Are you implying (that) I am wrong?

words.definitionEn: to introduce a new law, rule, tax, etc.; to order that a rule, punishment, etc. be used
words.exampleEn: impose something, The UN Security Council imposed sanctions in 1992.

words.definitionEn: a word or statement saying sorry for something that has been done wrong or that causes a problem
words.exampleEn: to offer/make/demand/accept an apology

words.definitionEn: if a person or thing impresses you, you admire them or it
words.exampleEn: impress (somebody), We interviewed a number of candidates but none of them impressed us.

words.definitionEn: admiring somebody/something because you think they are particularly good, interesting, etc.
words.exampleEn: I must admit I am impressed.

words.definitionEn: something that encourages you to do something
words.exampleEn: There is no incentive for people to save fuel.

words.definitionEn: a unit for measuring length or height, equal to 2.54 centimetres. There are 12 inches in a foot.
words.exampleEn: She's a few inches taller than me.

words.definitionEn: something that happens, especially something unusual or unpleasant
words.exampleEn: His bad behaviour was just an isolated incident.

words.definitionEn: the money that a person, a region, a country, etc. earns from work, from investing money, from business, etc.
words.exampleEn: Average household income fell slightly.

words.definitionEn: to include something so that it forms a part of something
words.exampleEn: incorporate something, The new car design incorporates all the latest safety features.

words.definitionEn: not accurate or true
words.exampleEn: incorrect information/spelling

words.definitionEn: more and more all the time
words.exampleEn: Tourism is playing an increasingly important role in the region's economy

words.definitionEn: easy to see or understand
words.exampleEn: Their devotion was apparent.

words.definitionEn: freedom from political control by other countries
words.exampleEn: Cuba gained independence from Spain in 1898.

words.definitionEn: a list of names or topics that are referred to in a book, etc., usually arranged at the end of a book in alphabetical order or listed in a separate file or book
words.exampleEn: Look it up in the index.

words.definitionEn: a remark or sign that shows that something is happening or what somebody is thinking or feeling
words.exampleEn: indication of something, They gave no indication of how the work should be done.
